title: Blepharoglossum rhombeum
taxonomy: Blepharoglossum rhombeum is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the spleenwort family. It belongs to the genus Blepharoglossum, a group of ferns native to Central and South America. Blepharoglossum rhombeum is a rhombic or heart-shaped fern with delicate fronds, typically found in shaded, humid environments. This species is often found growing in association with other epiphytes and vines. As a member of the Polypodiaceae family, Blepharoglossum rhombeum is closely related to other species of spleenworts and polypody ferns.

intro: Blepharoglossum rhombeum, commonly known as the rhombic lip fern or rhombic lip-fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and the improvement of respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Blepharoglossum rhombeum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and other secondary metabolites, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in infusions and decoctions to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
